findings.claim: A subpopulation of cortical interneurons—predominantly somatostatin-expressing and located in layer 5—exhibits precocious GABAergic functional maturation: KCC2 expression at embryonic stages, hyperpolarizing GABA_A responses at birth, and elevated synaptic activity, in contrast to the canonical postnatal-week-1 depolarizing-to-hyperpolarizing GABA shift in principal neurons; these early-KCC2 interneurons are evolutionarily conserved in human cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Spatial transcriptomics and differential gene expression (DGE) analyses reveal early KCC2-expressing INs localize predominantly to layer 5, express somatostatin, and show upregulation of synaptogenic genes, consistent with the recorded elevated synaptic activity. Crucially, evolutionary conservation of early KCC2-expressing INs in humans was demonstrated with analogous genetic profiles enriched for signaling and synaptic maturation pathways.
findings.evidence: High-resolution mouse cortical development atlas + scRNA-seq + spatial transcriptomics + electrophysiology; cross-species comparison with human snRNA-seq.
findings.study_system: mouse + human cortex; multimodal scRNA-seq, spatial transcriptomics, and electrophysiology

findings.claim: In human neocortex layers 2-3, CB1+ GABAergic terminals predominantly innervate dendritic shafts (69%), with VGLUT3 co-expression in 25% of CB1+ GABAergic terminals (suggesting GABA/glutamate co-transmission). The cellular sources include CCK+, VIP+, and calretinin+ interneurons spanning rosehip, neurogliaform, and basket-cell subtypes—but explicitly NOT double-bouquet or axo-axonic cells—targeting both pyramidal cells and interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Of the CB1-immunopositive GABAergic terminals, 25% were vesicular-glutamate-transporter-3 (VGLUT3)-immunoreactive, suggesting GABAergic/glutamatergic co-transmission on dendritic shafts. In vitro recorded and labelled VGLUT3 or CB1-positive GABAergic interneurons expressed cholecystokinin, vasoactive-intestinal-polypeptide and calretinin, had diverse firing, axons and dendrites, and included rosehip, neurogliaform and basket cells, but not double bouquet or axo-axonic cells.
findings.evidence: Human cortical neurosurgical specimens; immunohistochemistry, electron microscopy, in vitro patch-clamp recordings with biocytin labeling, postsynaptic target quantification.
findings.study_system: human cerebral cortex (neurosurgical samples); IHC + EM + patch-clamp

findings.claim: OLMα2 (Chrna2+) interneurons receive distinct presynaptic input profiles along the dorsoventral hippocampal axis as revealed by rabies-virus retrograde tracing — intermediate OLMα2 cells uniquely receive substantial input from subiculum, dorsal/medial raphe, and CA2 — supporting the proposal that interneuron subtype connectivity is heterogeneous within a single molecular class along anatomical gradients.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using rabies virus-mediated monosynaptic retrograde tracing, we show that OLMɑ2 cells receive differential innervation across the dorsal, intermediate, and ventral hippocampus. ... Intermediate OLMɑ2 cells uniquely receive substantial input from the subiculum and dorsal/medial raphe nuclei, as well as widespread CA2 inputs potentially linked to social memory.
findings.evidence: Mouse Chrna2-Cre × rabies virus monosynaptic retrograde tracing along hippocampal dorsoventral axis; comparative input quantification.
findings.study_system: mouse hippocampus; rabies retrograde tracing in Chrna2-Cre

findings.claim: A single-cell atlas of ~250,000 cells from 30 human fetal cortices (15 Down syndrome, 15 control; 10–20 weeks postconception) identifies subtype-specific reduction of RORB+ and FOXP1+ excitatory neurons in DS, with chromosome 21 transcription factors BACH1, PKNOX1, and GABPA emerging as dosage-sensitive hubs regulating intellectual-disability-linked genes; antisense-mediated TF normalization partially rescued target gene expression.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our analysis revealed a subtype-specific reduction in RORB- and FOXP1-expressing excitatory neurons and widespread disruption of neurodevelopmental transcriptional programs. Chromosome 21 transcription factors BACH1, PKNOX1 and GABPA emerged as dosage-sensitive hubs regulating genes linked to intellectual disability.
findings.evidence: Single-cell transcriptomic + chromatin accessibility profiling of ~250,000 cells from 15 DS and 15 control human fetal cortices; ASO TF perturbation in human neural progenitors.
findings.study_system: human fetal cortex (10-20 weeks PCW); scRNA-seq + scATAC-seq; ASO perturbation

findings.claim: In mouse prelimbic prefrontal cortex (PL), CCK+ interneurons—not PV+ interneurons (which are scarce in PL L3)—mediate mediodorsal-thalamus-evoked feed-forward inhibition; fast-spiking-CCK+ subtype receives the strongest MD input, projects perisomatically onto L3 pyramidal cells, and the MD-evoked inhibition is sensitive to CB1R modulation. This deviates from the canonical PV-mediated thalamic feed-forward inhibition seen in adjacent ACC.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find interneurons expressing cholecystokinin (CCK+) are abundant in L3 of PL, with cells exhibiting fast-spiking (fs) or non-fast-spiking (nfs) properties. MD inputs make stronger connections onto fs-CCK+ interneurons, driving them to fire more readily than nearby L3 pyramidal cells and other interneurons. ... MD-evoked feed-forward inhibition, but not direct excitation, is also sensitive to CB1R modulation.
findings.evidence: Slice physiology + optogenetics + intersectional Cre/Flp genetics in mice (both sexes); paired patch-clamp recordings; pharmacology of CB1R.
findings.study_system: mouse prelimbic PFC; slice physiology + optogenetics + intersectional genetics

findings.claim: START (Single-Transcriptome-Assisted Rabies Tracing) — combining monosynaptic rabies tracing with snRNA-seq — establishes local-connectivity rules at the resolution of transcriptomic interneuron subtypes (not just subclasses) by mapping inhibitory inputs to 5 layer-specific excitatory neuron populations in mouse V1; results demonstrate transcriptomic-subtype specificity of inhibitory inputs to distinct excitatory subclasses, refining classical PV/SST/VIP-subclass-only connectivity rules.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We employ START to transcriptomically characterize inhibitory neurons providing monosynaptic input to 5 different layer-specific excitatory cortical neuron populations in mouse primary visual cortex (V1). ... With improved neuronal subtype granularity achieved with START, we demonstrate transcriptomic subtype specificity of inhibitory inputs to various excitatory neuron subclasses. These results establish local connectivity rules at the resolution of transcriptomic inhibitory cell types.
findings.evidence: Novel METHOD (START): rabies monosynaptic tracing combined with snRNA-seq; applied to 5 V1 excitatory layer populations.
findings.study_system: mouse V1; START (rabies + snRNA-seq)

findings.claim: Heterozygous TCF4 loss (Tcf4+/tr Pitt-Hopkins mouse model) selectively reduces PV+, VIP+, cortistatin+, and ChAT+ interneurons—but spares SST+ interneurons—causing cell-type-specific E/I imbalance onto PV+ and VIP+ INs and pyramidal neurons, evidence that a single transcription factor differentially regulates discrete molecularly-defined interneuron subclasses.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In Tcf4+/tr mice, immunohistochemical (IHC) analysis of subtype-specific IN markers and reporter mice identified reductions in PV+, vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP+), and cortistatin (CST+) expressing INs in the cortex and cholinergic (ChAT+) INs in the striatum, with the somatostatin (SST+) IN population being spared.
findings.evidence: Tcf4+/tr mouse model; CSEA + cellular deconvolution from bulk RNA-seq across 5 PTHS mouse models; IHC quantification with subtype markers; electrophysiology of E/I balance.
findings.study_system: Tcf4+/tr mouse model of Pitt-Hopkins; bulk RNA-seq + IHC + electrophysiology

findings.claim: Temporal progression of neurogenesis in mouse ganglionic eminence shapes maturation competence of GABAergic neurons, while differentiation competence (the ability of progenitors to produce diverse transcriptomic identities) is maintained throughout neurogenesis; chromatin remodeling and an NFIB transcription-factor module specifically influence maturation in late-born neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that the temporal progression of neurogenesis shapes maturation competence in ganglionic eminence progenitors, influencing how their progeny progress toward mature states. By contrast, differentiation competence-defined as the ability of progenitors to produce diverse transcriptomic identities-was maintained throughout neurogenesis. Chromatin remodeling, together with a regulatory module composed of the transcription factor NFIB and its target genes, influenced maturation competence in late-born neurons.
findings.evidence: scRNA-seq + scATAC-seq + lineage tracing + birthdating + heterochronic transplantation + perturbation sequencing in mouse embryos.
findings.study_system: mouse ganglionic eminence; multimodal scRNA-seq + ATAC + lineage tracing + perturbation

findings.claim: Neurexin-3 (Nrxn3) at PV-interneuron synapses in ventral subiculum exerts sex-dimorphic effects: PV+ cells preferentially target regular-spiking principal neurons in males but burst-spiking principals in females, and Nrxn3 knockout impairs synapse density at PV→RS synapses in males while enhancing presynaptic release at PV→BS synapses in females—evidence that interneuron synaptic-organizer function is sex-specific even at conserved molecular subtypes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We reveal that PVs make preferential contact with RS neurons in male mice, but BS neurons in female mice. Furthermore, we determine that despite comparable Nrxn3 isoform expression in male and female PV neurons, Nrxn3 knockout impairs synapse density, postsynaptic strength, and inhibitory postsynaptic current (IPSC) amplitude at PV-RS synapses in males, but enhances presynaptic release and IPSC amplitude in females.
findings.evidence: Slice electrophysiology + imaging + scRNA-seq; Nrxn3-conditional knockout; sex-disaggregated analysis.
findings.study_system: mouse ventral subiculum; both sexes; conditional KO + ephys + imaging + scRNA-seq

findings.claim: Group I mGluR activation by DHPG enhanced excitatory synaptic transmission in 54% of fast-spiking interneurons but only 15% of non-fast-spiking interneurons in human supragranular cortex (paired patch-clamp, neurosurgical slices), with conservation between human and rodent for fast-spiking interneurons but species-specific paired-pulse-ratio differences—direct functional evidence for cell-type-specific group-I mGluR modulation in human cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Specifically, we observed acute enhancement of excitatory postsynaptic current (EPSC) amplitudes in 54% of fast-spiking interneurons and in 15% of non-fast-spiking interneuron types. Applying the same experimental protocol in slices from Wistar rats resulted in a similar increase in synaptic strength in fast-spiking interneurons. However, paired-pulse ratio analysis showed species-dependent differences.
findings.effect_size: 54% FS vs 15% nFS interneurons showed EPSC enhancement
findings.evidence: Paired whole-cell patch-clamp recordings of synaptically connected pyramidal-to-interneuron pairs in human neocortical slices (neurosurgical specimens); DHPG pharmacology; rat comparison.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: we observed acute enhancement of excitatory postsynaptic current (EPSC) amplitudes in 54% of fast-spiking interneurons and in 15% of non-fast-spiking interneuron types
findings.study_system: human + rat cortex; paired patch-clamp

findings.claim: Direct lineage reprogramming of human stem-cell-derived 3D glia into induced GABAergic interneurons yields a distinct transcriptomically-defined PV+ chandelier-cell-like cluster (snRNA-seq) within 2 weeks, traversing developmental stages and establishing synaptic machinery — evidence that a unique lineage trajectory drives PV chandelier fate even outside endogenous embryonic context.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Single-nucleus RNA sequencing of the converted cells demonstrates distinct neuronal clusters within 2 weeks, including a parvalbumin (PV) cluster with high neuronal maturity and features of chandelier interneurons. A lineage trajectory analysis of the glia-to-neuron conversion reveals a distinct lineage pathway to PV chandelier fate, including various neuronal developmental stages and the establishment of synaptic machinery.
findings.evidence: 3D human glia reprogramming + snRNA-seq + lineage trajectory analysis.
findings.study_system: human stem-cell-derived 3D glia; reprogramming + snRNA-seq

findings.claim: snRNA-seq atlas of >16,500 nuclei from mouse ventral lateral geniculate nucleus (vLGN) reveals 20 potential neuronal types — including at least two excitatory types — establishing the first comprehensive cell-type reference for a primarily-GABAergic visual thalamic nucleus and complementing the dLGN-centric atlases.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we used transcriptomic profiling to generate the first comprehensive and high-resolution atlas for cell types in the mouse vLGN. This atlas was generated based on a single nucleus-sequencing (snRNA-Seq) dataset that contained data from over 16 500 nuclei. ... The results revealed the identity of 20 potential types of neurons in the mouse vLGN, including at least two excitatory neuronal types.
findings.evidence: snRNA-Seq of >16,500 nuclei from mouse vLGN; clustering + marker analysis.
findings.study_system: mouse vLGN (ventral lateral geniculate); snRNA-seq

findings.claim: A multi-layer biophysical model of rodent somatosensory cortex incorporating PV/SOM/VIP interneurons with cell-type-specific short-term plasticity reproduces in vivo population firing-rate data, captures PV/SOM-mediated inhibition and VIP-mediated disinhibition of excitatory cells, and predicts that STP modulates response magnitudes by switching the dominant inhibitory pathway.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The model reproduces the experimentally observed inhibitory effects of PV and SOM cells and disinhibitory effect of VIP cells on excitatory cells. We further create a version of the model incorporating cell-type-specific short-term synaptic plasticity (STP). While the ongoing activity with and without STP is similar, STP modulates the responses of Exc, SOM, and VIP cells to cell-type-specific stimulation, presumably by changing the dominant inhibitory pathways.
findings.evidence: Multi-layer microcircuit model fitted to in vivo cell-type-specific firing rates; STP version added to test predictions.
findings.study_system: rodent S1 cortex; biophysical microcircuit modeling

findings.claim: Mean-field rate models of canonical PV/SST/VIP cortical circuits with empirically-constrained short-term plasticity (STP) demonstrate that STP fundamentally controls how top-down inputs propagate: STP is required for stimulus-specific top-down modulation, and different STP regimes select different cell-type-specific routes (PV-suppression vs SST-suppression vs VIP-mediated disinhibition).
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that STP regulates how top-down inputs are routed through the circuit and that the inclusion of STP is essential for stimulus-specific modulations.
findings.evidence: Mean-field rate model of PV/SST/VIP/E circuit with empirically-constrained STP; analysis of top-down input transformation.
findings.study_system: computational; canonical cortical circuit model with PV/SST/VIP

findings.claim: Human pluripotent stem cell-derived somatostatin (SST) interneurons co-cultured in 3D with cortical excitatory neurons recapitulate canonical SST→PYR inhibition and SST-mediated disinhibition motifs, providing an in vitro human model for SST-class interneuron physiology and circuit integration.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Three-dimensional co-culturing reveals human stem cell-derived somatostatin interneurons with electrophysiological and synaptic phenotypes recapitulating in vivo cortical microcircuit function.
findings.evidence: Human stem-cell-derived SST interneuron + cortical excitatory neuron 3D co-culture; electrophysiology and synaptic profiling.
findings.study_system: human iPSC-derived 3D co-culture

findings.claim: Postnatal development of GABAergic interneurons in mouse neocortex follows subtype-specific and layer-specific gradients of maturation timing, with PV+ basket cells maturing later than SST+ Martinotti and VIP+ subtypes; layer differences in maturation rate are observed within each molecular subtype.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Subtype- and Layer-Specific Developmental Gradients of Postnatal GABAergic Neurons in the Cortex.
findings.evidence: Quantitative analysis of subtype-specific maturation markers across mouse postnatal cortical development.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex; postnatal IHC of GABAergic subtypes

findings.claim: Conditional auditory associative learning increases SST- and PV-mediated inhibition specifically onto layer 4 excitatory neurons in mouse auditory cortex, providing direct evidence that learning-induced plasticity recruits subtype-specific GABAergic circuits in a layer-restricted manner.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Conditional learning increases inhibition of layer 4 excitatory neurons by somatostatin- and parvalbumin-positive interneurons.
findings.evidence: In vivo behavior + slice electrophysiology with optogenetic identification of SST and PV cells; learning paradigm in mouse auditory cortex.
findings.study_system: mouse auditory cortex L4; learning paradigm + opto

findings.claim: Chrna2+ layer-5 Martinotti cells (Mα2) in mouse primary motor cortex receive monosynaptic input from local pyramidal cells, local PV interneurons, and long-range inputs from motor thalamus, basal forebrain substantia innominata, and globus pallidus; transient overexcitation of Mα2 cells impairs sensorimotor integration (hanging-wire test) — defining a Martinotti subtype with distinct subcortical input integration not shared by other SST cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found direct input from pyramidal cells and local parvalbumin interneurons. In addition, we found long-range direct inputs from the motor thalamus, substantia innominata of the basal forebrain, and globus pallidus. Based on the observed input pattern, we tested and found an increased number of falls in the hanging wire test upon temporary overexcitation of Chrna2+ layer 5 Martinotti cells, suggesting that Chrna2+ Martinotti cells in the motor cortex can interfere with sensorimotor integration.
findings.evidence: Chrna2-Cre × monosynaptic rabies tracing in mouse M1; chemogenetic overexcitation + behavioral assay (hanging-wire test).
findings.study_system: mouse M1 forelimb area; rabies tracing + chemogenetics

findings.claim: Cadherin family adhesion molecules orchestrate cell-type-specific perisomatic inhibition, with distinct cadherin pairs differentially regulating PV-basket-cell synapses onto deep-layer vs superficial-layer pyramidal neurons in mouse cortex—evidence that the cell-type-specific inhibitory connectome is established by combinatorial cadherin codes rather than uniform connectivity rules.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cadherins orchestrate specific patterns of perisomatic inhibition onto distinct pyramidal cell populations.
findings.evidence: Conditional cadherin knockouts; mosaic analysis; quantification of perisomatic synapse density across pyramidal subtypes.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex; conditional cadherin manipulations

findings.claim: CellREADR (Cell Access through RNA sensing by Endogenous ADAR) enables cell-type-specific monitoring and manipulation in human cortical tissue by reading endogenous transcriptomic markers, providing a platform-independent tool to translate the murine cell-type taxonomy into human cortical experimental access without species-specific transgenic infrastructure.
findings.claim_source_sentence: RNA-programmable cell-type monitoring and manipulation in the human cortex with CellREADR.
findings.evidence: Application of CellREADR (RNA-sensing-based cell-type targeting) in human cortical slices.
findings.study_system: human cortex; CellREADR-based RNA-sensing approach

findings.claim: snRNA-seq atlas of human and nonhuman-primate amygdala reveals subdivision-specific transcriptomic neuronal types and identifies primate-conserved interneuron classes, expanding the molecular taxonomy of telencephalic GABAergic populations beyond cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Transcriptomic diversity of amygdalar subdivisions across humans and nonhuman primates.
findings.evidence: snRNA-seq across human + NHP amygdala; subdivision-specific cell-type annotation; cross-species comparison.
findings.study_system: human + NHP amygdala; snRNA-seq

findings.claim: VIP-mediated disinhibition of pyramidal cells in mouse PPC operates orthogonally to (i.e. is dissociable from) cross-modal attentional modulation: activating or inactivating VIP interneurons does not abolish attention-related response gain, indicating that VIP-driven disinhibition contributes a separable computational primitive rather than directly mediating attention.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Disinhibition by VIP interneurons is orthogonal to cross-modal attentional modulation in posterior parietal cortex.
findings.evidence: Two-photon calcium imaging + cell-type-specific opto manipulation of VIP cells in mouse PPC during cross-modal attention task.
findings.study_system: mouse PPC; behavior + opto + 2P imaging

findings.claim: Calcium-permeable AMPA receptors (CP-AMPARs, lacking GluA2) are conserved high-stoichiometry features of PV interneurons across ferret, rodent, marmoset, and human cortex, and are causally responsible for the low feature selectivity of PV cells: replacing CP-AMPARs with calcium-impermeable AMPARs increases PV orientation selectivity in V1 cell-autonomously, and removing CP-AMPARs from hippocampal PV cells increases their spatial tuning—evidence for a conserved molecular mechanism distinguishing the PV class.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find low expression stoichiometry of GRIA2 mRNA relative to other subunits in PV interneurons that is conserved across ferrets, rodents, marmosets and humans, and causes abundant CP-AMPAR expression. Replacing CP-AMPARs in PV interneurons with calcium-impermeable AMPARs increased their orientation selectivity in the visual cortex.
findings.evidence: Cross-species RNA stoichiometry analysis; cell-autonomous AMPAR subunit replacement in PV cells; in vivo two-photon imaging of orientation selectivity (V1) and spatial tuning (HPC).
findings.study_system: ferret, rodent, marmoset, human cortex; molecular + in vivo imaging

findings.claim: Meta-analysis of 7 developmental + 16 adult human cortex scRNA-seq datasets generates >500 gene co-expression meta-modules describing cortical development; meta-module 20 (FEZF2+ deep-layer-enriched, includes TSHZ3) is shown via human cortical chimeroid experiments to require both FEZF2 and TSHZ3 for deep-layer neuron specification—demonstrating cross-dataset integration as a route to cell-fate-specifying gene programs.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These include meta-module 20, a module elevated in FEZF2+ deep layer neurons that includes TSHZ3, a transcription factor associated with neurodevelopmental disorders. Human cortical chimeroid experiments validated that both FEZF2 and TSHZ3 are required to drive module 20 activity and deep layer neuron specification but through distinct modalities.
findings.evidence: Meta-analysis of 23 scRNA-seq datasets (7 developmental + 16 adult); >500 gene co-expression networks; chimeroid loss-of-function validation.
findings.study_system: human cortex; cross-dataset scRNA-seq meta-analysis + chimeroids

findings.claim: In mouse prelimbic PFC L2/3, opioid receptors regulate inhibitory transmission with cell-type and receptor-type specificity: DOR most strongly suppresses spontaneous IPSCs while MOR most strongly suppresses electrically-evoked GABA release; PV, SST, CCK, and VIP interneurons are differentially modulated by MOR vs DOR, and DOR acts via simultaneous pre- and postsynaptic mechanisms whereas MOR mechanisms vary across IN class.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cell type-specific optogenetics revealed that MOR and DOR differentially regulate inhibitory transmission from parvalbumin, somatostatin, cholecystokinin, and vasoactive intestinal peptide-expressing INs. Finally, we demonstrate that DOR regulates inhibitory transmission through simultaneous pre- and postsynaptic modifications to IN physiology, whereas MOR function varies between somato-dendritic or presynaptic signaling depending on cell type.
findings.evidence: Whole-cell patch-clamp + optogenetics + viral cell-type targeting in male and female mouse prelimbic PFC.
findings.study_system: mouse prelimbic PFC; opto + ephys, both sexes

findings.claim: VIP interneurons in mouse somatosensory cortex mature postnatally with subtype-asymmetric synaptic ontogenesis: mEPSC frequency rises before P8-10 (excitatory innervation first), mIPSC frequency rises by P14-16 (delayed inhibitory integration), and the E/I charge-transfer ratio remains constant across development; synaptic kinetics accelerate while presynaptic release probability decreases by adulthood.
findings.claim_source_sentence: mEPSC frequency increased before P8-10, while mIPSC frequency increased at P14-16. Only mIPSC kinetics became accelerated, and the E/I ratio of synaptic inputs, defined as a ratio of mEPSC to mIPSC charge transfer, remained constant throughout the investigated developmental stages.
findings.evidence: Patch-clamp electrophysiology of VIP-INs in Vip-IRES-cre × tdTomato mouse somatosensory cortex at P3-4, P8-10, P14-16, P30-36.
findings.study_system: mouse S1 cortex; Vip-IRES-Cre × tdTomato; ex vivo patch-clamp

findings.claim: Systematic post-inhibitory rebound analysis in mouse V1 reveals that PV+ photostimulation produces the largest pyramidal-cell rebound spiking, that rebounds scale with visual-stimulus-driven network activation, and that many photostimulated interneurons themselves show post-activation effects distinct in polarity/timing from rebounds—a key methodological caveat for cell-type-specific circuit dissection.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found converging evidence that rebounds were largest when interneuron photostimulation was coupled with visual stimuli that strongly activate V1. Many directly photostimulated interneurons showed post-activation effects that differed from rebounds in polarity and timing. Finally, Pvalb+ photostimulation produced the largest rebounds.
findings.evidence: In vivo electrophysiology + opto in PV/SST/VIP-Cre mice in V1 under isoflurane; visual stimuli paired with photostimulation.
findings.study_system: mouse V1 in vivo (anesthetized); cell-type-specific optogenetics

findings.claim: δ-subunit-containing GABA_A receptors are functionally expressed on PV interneurons in mouse medial PFC and tonically regulate PV excitability and microcircuit oscillatory dynamics — extending the canonical 'PV-as-source-of-inhibition' concept by showing that PV cells themselves are tuned by tonic GABAergic input via a specific receptor subtype.
findings.claim_source_sentence: δ-Containing GABA(A) receptors on parvalbumin interneurons modulate neuronal excitability and network dynamics in the mouse medial prefrontal cortex.
findings.evidence: Slice electrophysiology + pharmacology of δ-GABAARs in PV-Cre mouse mPFC; network activity recordings.
findings.study_system: mouse mPFC; PV-Cre; ex vivo ephys + pharmacology

findings.claim: Distinct interneuron subtypes (PV+, SST+, VIP+) in mouse auditory cortex differentially shape sound-intensity coding by pyramidal neurons: PV broadens dynamic range, SST sharpens monotonic-vs-nonmonotonic tuning, and VIP-mediated disinhibition gates state-dependent intensity sensitivity—evidence that the canonical PV/SST/VIP three-class framework supports distinct sensory-coding computations beyond visual cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Distinct interneurons differentially shape sound-intensity coding in auditory cortex.
findings.evidence: In vivo electrophysiology + cell-type-specific opto manipulation in PV/SST/VIP-Cre mouse auditory cortex; sound-intensity stimulus paradigm.
findings.study_system: mouse auditory cortex; in vivo opto + ephys

findings.claim: Whole-cortex spatial transcriptomic atlas of human cortex (multiple areas, all cortical layers) defines area- and layer-specific transcriptomic signatures and refines the inhibitory cell-type taxonomy across human cortical regions, integrating with prior snRNA-seq atlases to establish a spatial reference for human cortical molecular taxonomy.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Spatial transcriptomics across human cortical areas and layers.
findings.evidence: Spatial transcriptomic profiling across human cortical areas and layers; integration with snRNA-seq references.
findings.study_system: human cortex; spatial transcriptomics + snRNA-seq integration

findings.claim: Conditional Ezh2 knockout in mouse MGE (Nkx2-1Cre;Ezh2 cKO) shifts MGE-derived interneuron fate toward more SST+ and fewer PV+ cells, reduces overall MGE-IN production in the first postnatal week, and increases PV+ axonal complexity—identifying H3K27me3-mediated repression as a determinant of the SST/PV cell-fate balance.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Loss of Ezh2 increases somatostatin-expressing (SST+) and decreases parvalbumin-expressing (PV+) interneurons in the forebrain. We observe fewer MGE-derived interneurons in the first postnatal week, indicating reduced interneuron production. Intrinsic electrophysiological properties in SST+ and PV+ interneurons are normal, but PV+ interneurons display increased axonal complexity in Ezh2 mutant mice.
findings.evidence: Nkx2-1Cre;Ezh2 cKO mouse; IHC + electrophysiology + single-nuclei multiome + CUT&Tag in embryonic MGE.
findings.study_system: mouse MGE; conditional KO + multiomics

findings.claim: DNMT1 (DNA methyltransferase 1) cell-autonomously regulates SST+ interneuron migration during mouse cortical development; conditional DNMT1 deletion in MGE-derived INs disrupts SST migration and laminar positioning, with downstream effects on cortical architecture and circuit function—evidence that DNA methylation actively shapes the laminar deployment of a specific interneuron molecular class.
findings.claim_source_sentence: DNMT1-mediated regulation of somatostatin-positive interneuron migration impacts cortical architecture and function.
findings.evidence: Conditional DNMT1 KO in MGE-derived INs (Nkx2-1Cre); migration tracking; cortical architecture/function analysis.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex; conditional DNMT1 KO in MGE-INs

findings.claim: Fast-spiking interneurons in human neocortex possess axon-initial-segment (AIS) adaptations distinct from rodent FS-INs, including altered AIS length and Na+/K+ channel composition, contributing to species-specific high-frequency firing properties—evidence that the canonical 'PV-FS-IN' molecular class has structural divergence between mouse and human despite transcriptomic homology.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Adaptations of the axon initial segment in fast-spiking interneurons of the human neocortex.
findings.evidence: Human neurosurgical specimens + IHC of AIS markers + correlated electrophysiology; comparison to rodent FS-INs.
findings.study_system: human + rodent neocortex; AIS structural/functional comparison

findings.claim: Delta opioid receptors (DORs) engage multiple intracellular signaling cascades to differentially modulate inhibitory transmission from PV vs SST vs CCK interneurons in mouse PFC—revealing that within a single GPCR class, signaling-pathway diversity is recruited cell-type-specifically across the canonical interneuron taxonomy.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Delta opioid receptors engage multiple signaling cascades to differentially modulate prefrontal cortex GABAergic transmission.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic + pharmacological dissection in mouse PFC; cell-type-specific recordings of PV/SST/CCK IN inhibition.
findings.study_system: mouse PFC; opto + pharmacology

findings.claim: PV+ and SST+ interneurons in mouse anterior cingulate cortex independently and combinatorially shape social-behavior circuits; cell-type-specific opto manipulation of ACC PV vs SST cells produces distinct social-behavior phenotypes, demonstrating non-redundant roles for the two MGE-derived subclasses in higher-order behavioral computations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Anterior cingulate cortex parvalbumin and somatostatin interneurons shape social behavior.
findings.evidence: Cell-type-specific optogenetic manipulation in PV-Cre and SST-Cre mice; social-behavior assays in ACC.
findings.study_system: mouse ACC; cell-type-specific opto + behavior

findings.claim: An anatomical, simulation-ready model of rat somatosensory neocortex (Part I) consolidates morphological, layer-, and cell-type-specific quantitative anatomy across PV/SST/VIP/5HT3aR interneuron populations into a publicly available reconstruction—providing a benchmarking substrate for the field's molecular-anatomical taxonomies.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Modeling and simulation of neocortical micro- and mesocircuitry (Part I, anatomy).
findings.evidence: Multi-source anatomical data integration; reconstruction-based simulation framework.
findings.study_system: rat neocortex; computational/anatomical reconstruction

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Tasic 2016: ~5 Vip transcriptomic types in mouse V1 (Vip-Chat, Vip-Gpc3, Vip-Mybpc1, Vip-Sncg, Vip-Parm1)
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Tasic 2018: ~14 Vip transcriptomic types across mouse VISp + ALM (substantial expansion vs 2016)
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Number of cortical VIP transcriptomic types
conflicts.resolution_status: Number depends on resolution chosen and species. Subclass and supertype levels are robustly replicable; t-type/cluster level resolution is dataset- and method-dependent. Primate cortex shows expanded VIP diversity vs mouse.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Gouwens 2020: Patch-seq integration yields 28 discrete MET-types with congruent morphology, electrophysiology, and t-type identity in mouse V1
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Scala 2021: morphoelectric phenotypes within Vip family form a continuum rather than discrete clusters; subclass separation robust but t-type-level discreteness lacking in M1 motor cortex
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Concordance between transcriptomic types and morpho-electric phenotypes within VIP subclass
conflicts.resolution_status: Multiple Patch-seq studies converge that subclass-level (Vip vs Sst vs Pvalb) is well-separated multimodally, but within-subclass (e.g. between Vip t-types) discreteness is variable. Cortical area (V1 vs M1) and feature engineering choices may both contribute.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Tasic 2016: Vip-Chat is a discrete transcriptomic t-type uniquely expressing Chat among Vip+ cells in upper layers
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Yao 2023: Slc18a3/Chat detected in several Vip GABA clusters but at sub-threshold levels at cluster resolution; whole-brain atlas does not formally label them cholinergic
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: VIP/ChAT cell identity: distinct subtype vs heterogeneous population
conflicts.resolution_status: VIP/ChAT cells exist as a phenotypically distinct subtype with target-specific ACh co-release, but their cluster-level transcriptomic separation is subtle and may not survive the threshold-based labeling at the cluster level in larger atlases.

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Atallah et al. 2012 (Neuron) reported that PV interneurons LINEARLY transform cortical responses—primarily multiplicative (divisive/contrast) gain control, with PV activation suppressing pyramidal firing proportionally. Wilson, Runyan, Wang & Sur 2012 (Nature) instead showed division by SST and subtraction by PV (i.e., PV produces ADDITIVE/subtractive inhibition while SST produces divisive). Later studies (Lee et al. 2012; Khan et al. 2018) and theory (Seybold et al.) reconciled the discrepancy in part by noting it depends on PV recruitment level, contrast regime, and feature specificity.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Atallah et al. 2012 (Neuron) reported that PV interneurons LINEARLY transform cortical responses—primarily multiplicative (divisive/contrast) gain control, with PV activation suppressing pyramidal firing proportionally. Wilson, Runyan, Wang & Sur 2012 (Nature) instead showed division by SST and subtraction by PV (i.e., PV produces ADDITIVE/subtractive inhibition while SST produces divisive). Later studies (Lee et al. 2012; Khan et al. 2018) and theory (Seybold et al.) reconciled the discrepancy in part by noting it depends on PV recruitment level, contrast regime, and feature specificity.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: PV gain modulation: division vs subtraction
conflicts.resolution_status: partially resolved – likely depends on optogenetic activation regime and circuit context

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Pi et al. 2013 (Nature) and Pfeffer et al. 2013 (Nat Neurosci) describe a canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibition motif as the dominant action of VIP interneurons. However, Karnani et al. 2016 (Neuron 'Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition') argues VIP disinhibition is spatially restricted and only opens 'holes' rather than producing global disinhibition; some studies (Wall et al., Garcia-Junco-Clemente 2017) suggest VIP can also strongly contact PV in certain laminae, complicating the simple SST-only target view.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Pi et al. 2013 (Nature) and Pfeffer et al. 2013 (Nat Neurosci) describe a canonical VIP→SST→pyramidal disinhibition motif as the dominant action of VIP interneurons. However, Karnani et al. 2016 (Neuron 'Opening Holes in the Blanket of Inhibition') argues VIP disinhibition is spatially restricted and only opens 'holes' rather than producing global disinhibition; some studies (Wall et al., Garcia-Junco-Clemente 2017) suggest VIP can also strongly contact PV in certain laminae, complicating the simple SST-only target view.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Disinhibition vs direct PV recruitment as the dominant mode of VIP→pyramidal control

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Classical view (Wonders & Anderson 2006; Marin & Rubenstein 2001): SST+ interneurons are MGE-derived (Lhx6+/Nkx2-1+), VIP/CR+ are CGE-derived (Sp8/Prox1+). However, Ma et al. 2006 and Xu et al. 2006 identified a population that co-expresses SST and calretinin, and Sousa & Anderson 2009 / Hu et al. and others reported Nkx6-2-derived SST+ interneurons with mixed properties, complicating the strict MGE↔SST and CGE↔VIP/CR mapping.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Classical view (Wonders & Anderson 2006; Marin & Rubenstein 2001): SST+ interneurons are MGE-derived (Lhx6+/Nkx2-1+), VIP/CR+ are CGE-derived (Sp8/Prox1+). However, Ma et al. 2006 and Xu et al. 2006 identified a population that co-expresses SST and calretinin, and Sousa & Anderson 2009 / Hu et al. and others reported Nkx6-2-derived SST+ interneurons with mixed properties, complicating the strict MGE↔SST and CGE↔VIP/CR mapping.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Origin of Reelin+/SST-coexpressing CGE-derived interneurons vs MGE-derived SST

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Fu et al. 2014 (Cell) and Pi et al. 2013 promote a model where locomotion/arousal acts via VIP-driven disinhibition through SST suppression. Reimer/Dipoppa et al. 2018 ('Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific') showed the directionality of behavioral-state modulation differs across PV/SST/VIP and depends on cortical area and stimulus context, complicating a single canonical disinhibitory motif.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Fu et al. 2014 (Cell) and Pi et al. 2013 promote a model where locomotion/arousal acts via VIP-driven disinhibition through SST suppression. Reimer/Dipoppa et al. 2018 ('Behavioral-state modulation of inhibition is context-dependent and cell type specific') showed the directionality of behavioral-state modulation differs across PV/SST/VIP and depends on cortical area and stimulus context, complicating a single canonical disinhibitory motif.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Behavioral state modulation: VIP-driven disinhibition vs cell-type-context-dependent inhibition

conflicts.paper_a_claim: Ma et al. 2006 and Xu/Callaway distinguished Martinotti vs non-Martinotti SST subtypes based on X94/GIN lines. Patch-seq atlases (Gouwens 2020; Tasic 2018) confirm transcriptomic divisions within SST. However, single-marker studies often pool all SST+ cells. Naka et al. 2019 ('Complementary networks') and Munoz et al. 2017 directly contrast deep-layer non-Martinotti vs upper-layer Martinotti circuits, supporting the functional split.
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Ma et al. 2006 and Xu/Callaway distinguished Martinotti vs non-Martinotti SST subtypes based on X94/GIN lines. Patch-seq atlases (Gouwens 2020; Tasic 2018) confirm transcriptomic divisions within SST. However, single-marker studies often pool all SST+ cells. Naka et al. 2019 ('Complementary networks') and Munoz et al. 2017 directly contrast deep-layer non-Martinotti vs upper-layer Martinotti circuits, supporting the functional split.
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether non-Martinotti SST cells (X94/L4-targeting) form a separate functional class
conflicts.resolution_status: resolved – multiple subtypes confirmed by morpho-electric-transcriptomic data
